tCL.definition: Delay from read command to first data availability.
tCL.stability_relevance: Often voltage and IC quality sensitive.

tRCD.definition: Delay between activating a row and issuing column access when split read/write values are unavailable.
tRCD.stability_relevance: Usually harder to tighten than tCL on DDR5.

tRCDRD.definition: Delay from row activate to read command.
tRCDRD.performance_relevance: Drives read row-miss estimate with tCL and tRP.

tRCDWR.definition: Delay from row activate to write command.
tRCDWR.stability_relevance: Often can differ from tRCDRD depending on platform.

tRP.definition: Time to precharge a bank before activating another row.
tRP.stability_relevance: Primary stability limiter on many profiles.

tRAS.definition: Minimum time a row remains active before precharge.
tRAS.performance_relevance: Workload dependent; not a direct application latency number.
tRAS.stability_relevance: Too low can violate row operation requirements.

tRC.definition: Activate-to-activate cycle for the same bank, commonly related to tRAS + tRP.
tRC.stability_relevance: Often derived or constrained by primaries.

tRFC.definition: Time after all-bank refresh before refreshed banks can resume normal access.
tRFC.performance_relevance: Large refresh penalty; visible in timing-derived refresh estimate.
tRFC.stability_relevance: Too low can create memory errors, especially with heat.

tRFC2.definition: Alternate refresh-cycle timing for fine granularity refresh modes.

tRFCsb.definition: DDR5 same-bank refresh duration affecting only the targeted same bank across bank groups.
tRFCsb.performance_relevance: DDR5-specific refresh behavior can reduce average lockout versus all-bank refresh.

tREFI.performance_relevance: Higher values reduce refresh frequency but increase retention risk.
tREFI.stability_relevance: Heat sensitive; high values need stability testing.
safe_notes: High tREFI is not automatically safe; DIMM temperature matters.

tRRDS.definition: Delay between activates to different bank groups in the short path.
tRRDS.stability_relevance: Too low can hurt training or stability.

tRRDL.definition: Delay between activates in a long/same-bank-group related path.

tFAW.definition: Window limiting how closely multiple activate commands may occur.
tFAW.stability_relevance: Too low can be unstable or counterproductive.

tWR.definition: Final write recovery delay before a precharge-related operation can safely follow a write.
tWR.performance_relevance: Scored as the final displayed tWR value, even when the BIOS uses a different control name.
tWR.stability_relevance: Too low can corrupt write recovery.
dependency_notes: tWR is often not the direct editable register; adjust tWRPRE
or the board vendor equivalent and re-check the final displayed tWR. On AM5
but final displayed tWR remains the value to compare.

tWTRS.performance_relevance: Scored as the final displayed tWTRS value, regardless of which BIOS control produced it.
tWTRS.stability_relevance: Often controlled indirectly on some platforms.
dependency_notes: tWTRS may be moved through tWRRD or vendor write-to-read controls; on AM5 it may be direct or derived. Change the available BIOS control
then compare the final tWTRS value.

tWTRL.performance_relevance: Scored as the final displayed tWTRL value, regardless of which BIOS control produced it.
dependency_notes: tWTRL may be affected by tWRRD or vendor write-to-read controls; on AM5 it may be direct or derived. Compare the final displayed tWTRL value.

tRTP.definition: Delay from read command to precharge.
tRTP.stability_relevance: Too low can fail stability tests.

tCWL.definition: Delay from write command to write data.
tCWL.stability_relevance: Often coupled to memory ratio and platform behavior.

tCKE.definition: Minimum CKE pulse width or power-state timing.
tCKE.performance_relevance: Low direct impact for most manual tuning.

tMOD.definition: Delay after mode register command before normal commands.

tXP.stability_relevance: Low unless power states are active.

tXSDLL.definition: Exit self-refresh timing when DLL-related behavior is exposed.

tWRRD.definition: Platform-exposed write-to-read delay control or family of controls.
tWRRD.performance_relevance: Useful mostly as the BIOS lever that can move final tWTR values on some boards.
tWRRD.stability_relevance: Too low may cause boot or training failures.
dependency_notes: Do not compare tWRRD across Intel and AMD as if it were always the same final timing. Use it to adjust the board
then score the final displayed tWTRS/tWTRL values.

tWRWR.definition: Delay between write commands in exposed tertiary timing set.

tRDRD.definition: Delay between read commands in exposed tertiary timing set.

tRDRDSD.definition: Same-DIMM read-to-read delay exposed by some BIOSes.

tRDRDDD.definition: Different-DIMM read-to-read delay exposed by some BIOSes.

tWRPRE.definition: Write-precharge related control that may determine final tWR on some BIOSes.
tWRPRE.performance_relevance: Useful mostly as the BIOS lever that can move final tWR.
tWRPRE.stability_relevance: Too low may break write recovery.
dependency_notes: Intel users may need to adjust tWRPRE or a vendor equivalent to change final tWR. AMD users may have direct tWR or a derived field. Compare the final displayed tWR value.

tPPD.definition: Vendor/platform-specific precharge power-down timing if exposed.
